summary: Tested Birth Summary Report feature (PDF generation). Backend APIs pass 100% (10/10 tests). CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG: The 'Report' download button is NOT being rendered in the BirthRecordSection component, even though the code exists. Backend PDF generation works correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Download Report button ('Report' text with download icon) is not rendering on the page. Code at lines 421-440 shows the button should appear when birthRecord exists, but DOM inspection shows 0 elements with 'Report' text while 'Edit' button is visible.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot trigger PDF download from frontend because the Download Report button is not rendered

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix BirthRecordSection.tsx - The Download Report button (lines 421-440) is not rendering even when birthRecord exists. Debug why the {birthRecord && (...)} condition is not rendering the button. The 'Edit' button in the same conditional block renders correctly.
Verify that the birthRecord state is properly set when data is fetched
Check if there's a render order or state timing issue preventing the button from appearing
critical_code_review_comments: BirthRecordSection.tsx line 421-440: The Report button JSX exists but doesn't render. Possible causes: 1) birthRecord might be falsy when it shouldn't be, 2) Conditional rendering issue, 3) CSS/styling hiding the element
The Edit button at line 431-438 renders correctly, suggesting the birthRecord condition is true but something specific to the downloadButton is failing
Consider adding testID prop instead of data-testid for React Native Web compatibility

success_rate.frontend: 50% (Birth Record section loads, but Download Report button missing)

seed_data_creation: Used existing test client TEST_PV_Client_200830 with birth record
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend birth-summary PDF and preview endpoints work correctly. The frontend BirthRecordSection component has the code for a 'Report' download button but it's not rendering. When navigating to client detail for TEST_PV_Client_200830 (client_id=client_a034be9c9748), scroll to Birth Record section - you'll see the Edit button but NOT the Report button. The button should be to the left of Edit button.
rca_of_the_issue: Root Cause: The Download Report button JSX exists in code (BirthRecordSection.tsx lines 421-440) within a {birthRecord && ...} conditional, but the button element is not present in the DOM. The Edit button in the same conditional renders correctly. Possible causes: 1) The button is being conditionally excluded elsewhere, 2) There's a component tree issue, 3) The birthRecord check passes for Edit but not for the outer conditional somehow. Reproduction: Login as midwife, go to Clients, click TEST_PV_Client_200830, scroll to Birth Record section - Edit button visible, Report button missing.
